data.content: Debug symbols downloaded with matching Build ID. Now let me symbolicate all the frames.

arguments.command:
# Place debug file properly for debuginfod-style lookup
mkdir -p /tmp/symbols/.build-id/1e
cp /tmp/symbols/_.debug /tmp/symbols/.build-id/1e/b39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be.debug

# Symbolicate all mono frames using addr2line with the debug file
# Offsets from the tombstone: #01-#07
ADDRS="0x222098 0x2241ac 0x1abc80 0x1abd14 0x16dfa4 0x16db6c 0x16de04"

echo "=== Symbolicated backtrace ==="
for addr in $ADDRS; do
  result=$(addr2line -C -f -e /tmp/symbols/_.debug "$addr" 2>&1)
  func=$(echo "$result" | head -1)
  loc=$(echo "$result" | tail -1)
  printf "  pc %-14s  %s at %s\n" "$addr" "$func" "$loc"
done

```

                                          at icall.c:6244

                                          at icall-def.h:202

                                          at interp/interp.c:2457
#04 pc 0x1abd14  libmonosgen-2.0.so      get_interp_to_native_trampoline
                                          at interp/interp.c:1732
#05 pc 0x16dfa4  libmonosgen-2.0.so      handle_isinst
                                          at type-checking.c:?
#06 pc 0x16db6c  libmonosgen-2.0.so      handle_isinst
                                          at type-checking.c:506
#07 pc 0x16de04  libmonosgen-2.0.so      handle_isinst
                                          at type-checking.c:528
#08 pc 0x307c    <anonymous JIT code>
```

**Summary:** The crash was a `SIGABRT` triggered by `Environment.FailFast` (abort message: "not combatible"). The call originates from `handle_isinst` (type-checking/casting logic in the Mono JIT), which goes through the interpreter trampoline (`do_icall`) into `FailFast`, which calls libc `abort()`. Frame #08 is JIT-compiled managed code (not symbolizable from native symbols alone).
